sentences.text: Hey, good morning, Matt, how are you?
Hey, good morning, how are you?
I'm doing good.
How about yourself?
Doing pretty good!
So far, so good.
Almost Friday, so… It's even better.
Compliance when we are a day away.
Yeah, right?
Well, we're on the stretch now.
I'll, … I'm guessing the week hasn't been, too stressful then for you.
No, no, we're good, yeah.
Everything's been really good so far, so… yeah.
So far, so it's a good week.
Nothing too crazy, no fires spur out, or so.
put out or anything like that so far, so… knock on wood.
So, we're doing good.
Good.
Hopefully, then today and tomorrow can kind of just roll into the weekend.
Nothing too crazy.
Absolutely, that's the plan.
Where, where are you based out of?
We are based out of Irvine, and then I live about 25 minutes from it in Chino Hills.
I typically just go to the office, like, maybe, like, once or twice a month, depending on if I'm needed.
But, usually I work from home, so….
Yeah, that's the best.
Yeah.
It definitely allows you to capitalize on, like, the 5 to 9, and then outside of.
For sure.
Absolutely.
So cool.
Like, I couldn't… I couldn't think about not working this schedule.
For sure, I agree.
Well, it's nice to meet you, and I'm glad that we could connect on this.
I've actually met with, Camille and Amy, ….
Yes.
Multiple times last year.
Okay.
Amy was a huge fan.
Camille, I think I got right up to, like, a contract with her that I think some things happened internally, and it kind of got pushed to the wayside in, in April.
So….
When I saw this come through, I was like, alright, cool.
Familiar company, would love to get feedback and kind of see, you know, what's been changing, internally.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, for sure.

The call today, pretty casual, just a back-and-forth conversation around, like, rent comp data, market analysis, … I'm pretty familiar with what you guys do, but I'll defer to you, and you can just walk me through the steps again, and then, I'll take you through the platform and show you, kind of, the different functionalities that we have.
How's that sound?
Yeah, sounds good.
Cool.

… So I think the last time that I was working with Amy and Camille, I believe your teams are still kind of doing all this data collection manually, but, I think at one point… I can't remember which one hinted at this, but I think you guys were trying to work with Appfolio on maybe, like, their market data tool.
Does that ring a bell?
Yeah, we're still… yeah, we partnered with Appfolio about 2 years ago, And I know there has been some recent, like.
obstacles that Camille has kind of, like, emphasized that she's not able to get certain data points.
Sure.

… So, I mean, even… even retouch… even, like, you know, retouching with her might even be a good thing, because I know she's, like, kind of looking… looking for something to… to get more information on, especially on our websites.
Yeah.
But….
I know we have a contract with Appfolio and our website, so I'm not sure how… that logistics would work out on her end.
She oversees all that marketing data information from our websites and all that kind of stuff, so that would definitely be more in her… a ball in her court.

But I know, like I said, she has emphasized that she is looking for more data points, especially when it comes from the traffic for her website, to see how that's going, just because it's kind of vague on the information that we get from Appfolio now, from what she's indicated.
So, yeah, the more information, the better.
Yeah, totally.
I definitely think, I mean, if the feedback from them was extremely positive, I'm gonna assume yours will be too, but, definitely be open and honest with me.
I'm curious, what, kind of, how do you take a look at the data?
So, for me, I'm more of, like… the soft… the software, like, kind of guru for all of our systems in our company.
So basically, aside from, like, the marketing stuff, Camille will put me and get me involved in it, just to see if, like, things are fitting good, or where we can put it at.
But essentially, anything that has to do with, like, new systems being implemented in our company, I'm pretty much, like, directly involved in it, and usually, like.
pretty much, like, a decision maker outside of, like, my, like, you know, president or director of operations, Amy.

But I work now, I work really, really closely with Amy on basically implementing procedures of the systems, or the just basically training people on it, too, as well, and then, when it comes to, like, certain data points, like, the only thing I'm really involved in is, like, if, like, is, like, that fully reporting, so I'm not really too involved when it comes to, like, the website marketing or anything like that, so that's all Camille.
Okay.
So, essentially, more or less the man behind the curtain.
Yeah, pretty much.
Basically, yeah, there you go.
Cool.
on it.
Yeah, for sure, that makes sense.
Okay, well, have you, spoken to, Camille or Amy about ApartmentIQ at all?
It's okay if not.
No, no, no, no.
I feel like I've heard the name before pop up with them, though, so, like, that's why when I did see the name, I was like, oh yeah, I'll just take a look, whatever.
So, didn't hurt.
And then, I'm curious, how much do you know about us, if anything at all?
I really don't know much, to be honest.
I kind of came in here purposely to just get a clean slate, to just not really, like, and have you kind of show me around.
So, yeah, but I really don't… I've seen you guys' name… I've seen the name pop up numerous occasions, but I just haven't really dove into it or anything like that myself.
Okay.
I'll give you some background information, and then I'll walk you through the platform, but … we've been around for the last 5 years.
Our parent company, Rentable, has been around for over a decade, so, we got some skin in the game, but essentially what ApartmentIQ does is, we do automate the market survey process for you.
So, I would say maybe over the last two, maybe a year and a half, two years, we've just been a market survey tool.
But landing some of the larger, enterprise, like, partners in the space, basically on all these calls, it seems like all these companies have a handful of different data products.
They don't necessarily love all of them.
They like bits and pieces of each one.
Fair.

And a lot of our partners requested, like, hey, you know, we use CoStar, we don't love it, we love the market surveys that you do, is there any way to do, like, a CoStar-esque, you know, reporting in here?
So we've built that out.

Then it was like, hey, you know, our acquisitions and development teams, like, we need a better way to have live rent comp data for target, assets, or just, like, new development in the ground, can you build something like that out?
And so we've done that.
So, instead of just being a market survey, we kind of can connect A to Z with your entire company.
So, three functionalities.
One of which is for the day-to-day operations, where you get a live data feed into your properties and each specific comp set that you're tracking.

And so, with that, when you log in, it'll be live as of today, but this is great because it'll let you leverage and see where each individual unit and floor plan are positioned within that specific comp set.
The nice thing about that is, since we've been around for the last 5 years, and we've been tracking this unit-level data for 5 years.
depending on who's taking a look at this data, you can aggregate it back to any given time frame that you'd like.
So you could look year over year, quarter over quarter, month over month, just depends on who's looking at the data.
Second functionality is really geared toward acquisitions or new development.
You can query our entire database and create these custom market surveys for any property that you don't currently own or manage.
Takes about 3 seconds.
All you need is the address or the property name, and you'll get a real-time market survey, right there, and that continues to update every single day as well.
And the last functionality is very similar to CoStar.
It's gonna be all market, sub-market level information.
You can drill down to the neighborhood, you can create custom boundaries, radiuses.
In that report, you will also get, the 5 years of historicals for seasonality and trends.

You'll get a new supply and construction pipeline broken down into different stages, and then you will also get demographic information, just to help you and your teams understand, kind of, the profiles of that market.
Okay.

So, the thought process here was try to become that one-stop shop and kind of piece together all these different data sources that all these companies are using, because it seems like everyone was trying to streamline everything and have it just in one place.
Yeah, sounds good.
All the data is, 100% publicly available.
It's all coming from the online leasing section of the property websites, so as long as a asset has online leasing, we can track it.
We… it doesn't have to be a customer of ours.
We are tracking about, I believe, 37 million unique units every single night, though.
And, … yeah, probably have, like, over, like, 800 partners at this point, so… Not going anywhere, not gonna get sold, anything like that.
If anything, we're looking to acquire companies, and we're also pushing out, new products, so….
Nice.
Yup.
Love it about.
Let me, share my screen.
I will walk you through it.
And if you have questions throughout… Fire those off.
So this is going to be the landing page.
Like I said, three different sections and functionalities.
We'll start with the My Properties.
This is going to be your owned or managed assets.
On the left-hand side here, research would be Acquisitions, new development.
Explore is going to be that, market, sub-market level data.
But when you and your teams log in, all of your properties get their own individual dashboard and reports, so if we want to drill into one, you just click View.
And then everything here will populate.
So up top, these are gonna be your main reports of navigation.
I'll walk you through each super high level.
But the way this works is it's defaulted to show you the data live.
Again, you can aggregate this back over any given time frame.
And then the layout is as such.
The blue property is going to be your subject property.
The rest across the top are gonna be comps that either you have, supplied us to add into the report.
Or, our system can recommend what comps we think you should track based off, like, an in-house algorithm.

The nice thing about this, though, is that you and your teams are in complete control of the comp set, so if you'll notice in the top right here, edit comps, if we click onto this, you can edit these as often as you'd like.
Two different viewpoints.
The map view is going to be more visual.
Blue pin is your property.
White painter comps in the set.
Black pin is gonna be assets that maybe you would like to add.
Okay.
On the left-hand side, it's a little bit more analytical.
We'll give each asset a comp score and list them out from high to low.
That's gonna be based off of a few metrics.
Distance, your bill, total units, rent matching, amenity matching, and property class matching, and then we'll spit out these scores here.
Nice thing about this is we make it super easy to kind of filter this.

Not all of the properties in a market are going to be relevant to you, so you can use the filters up top here and hone in on exactly what sort of assets that you're trying to take a look at, and then those are the ones that will populate.
Cool.
Any sort of questions here?
No.
Looks good.
Awesome.
Now, I'll walk you through the reporting.
So just know on any report, everything can be filtered by a specific, asset.
You could also filter all the data down by, like, bedroom, bath, rent, availability, unit style, BTR, like, property class.
And then for the market survey specifically here.
If you click on Customize Metrics, there is a handful of metrics that you can either turn on or off.
That's user-specific, so I always recommend going there, turn those on.
But, I'll walk you through it.
So, again, this updates every single day, so when you log in, you're looking at today's information, but we could show you things like, total units, link you straight to the Google reviews.

We can show you, rent, both gross price per square foot, net effective, net effective price per square foot, and the reason that we can do that is because we're collecting the asking rents from the website, we're also scrubbing all concessions, whether it's a ribbon, pop-up, banner.
text, and then we are then implementing that to the either unit and or floor plan.
So we're collecting concessions every single day as well, and then we can also show you things like occupancy, leased exposure, availability, applications, and leases.

And the thing that I like most about this data tool is since everything is coming from 100% publicly available sources, if there's any sort of, like, metric, or calculation where you're like, hey, where's this coming from?
We can always trace it back to the source, and then you can even take it a step further, and you can drill down into pretty much all of these.
So, for any sort of units that we're saying are available.
You could click into it here, and we'll show you all those units.
So, the exact unit number, floor plan, size, square footage, status, date available.
price, net effective, gross, any sort of concession details attached to it.
So, that's, something there that I really like.
Same thing with, like, applications or leases.
You could click into any of these, and you'll see the exact units that we're referencing.
So we try to be as transparent as possible.

Could even take it a step further, where if you click into any of the property cards up top, then if we toggle on all units, since we've been tracking all of these properties for 5 years, we've essentially seen every single unit come and go.
So, Canada Music Grow has 430 units, we toggle on all units, I can show you the unit mix, and then down below here is the status of all 430 units.
Cool.
Nice thing about this, too, is you can track the price history from, like, a floor plan perspective, or each individual unit, if you drill down into it.
We'll show you both the asking rent, the net effective, and the concession amount attached.
Any sort of gap in the data just means there's no S3s available.
Okay.
And you can do that all the way down.
Yep.
… Next is gonna be pricing and availability.
This is at a quick glance.
We can get extremely granular with price in the next report, but here we'll give you all the key metrics that you can sort from high to low, or low to high at a click of a button.

This will just show you, from a… kind of like a top-down look, how are our studios, 1s, twos, or 3s positioned within this comp set, just by sorting any of the metrics from high to low, or low to high.
Third section is gonna be, fees and deposits.
So we keep pointing in the right direction there, and then we'll show you the different amenities.
It has a blue checkmark, that means that the unit has it, and then below that is going to be at the community level.
So again, blue checkmark means that the, the community itself has that.
Now, something that I think you'll like here… Oh, I'm getting ahead of myself.
Just know all this can be exported to PDF or Excel, depending on whichever you prefer.
It can be automated to hit inboxes, as often as you'd like, so that's a great way to kind of share this reporting outside of the tool with just different individuals in your organization.
But, next one here… This is probably my favorite.
We'll sort this to 3 months, but this is going to be our floor plan reporting.

So we list out each unique floor plan, in the comp set from square footage, low to high, and then if you follow the data, you'll get the unit mix, leased availability, and rent trends over the given time frame that you've selected.
So, this is great for being able to price out your floor plans, because not all one-bedrooms compete with other one-bedrooms.
And so, if we do an example here, Candy Music Row, let's go to the ones, … So they have about 17 different one-bedrooms.
The A9 makes up of about 11.
5%, the A4 makes up of about 10.
most likely you're going to be comping out against those, so this report can just cancel out the noise.
It makes things a lot more easier, and again, you can drill into any of these, see the exact units that we're referencing, or the price history.
So that's kind of the granular look.
If you want the roll-ups, toggle on bed type, we'll give you the averages.
So you'll get the same data here.
Unit mix, least availability, rent trends, and then, … units.
I'll skip this, but just know, this is a massive data dump.
It's every single individual unit that is currently available in the comp set, and or has leased over the last 30 days.
… Most people kind of export that, create their own pivot tables, it's a massive data dump.
But I'll walk you through the historicals, just set this to monthly.
So I think this is great for seasonality and trends.
We'll show you both, rent, gross, and net effective.
You could take a look at this from, like, an individual property perspective.
Or you could take a look at, like, the market as a whole and the comp median.
What I like is we'll have graphs and overlays, but below each one, we'll give you the raw data, powering it.
And then again, if you click into it, we'll show you those units and all the data that you need to reference for the reporting purposes.
So this would be the historicals for rent.
We'll show you concession value history as well.
You could take a look at that as the entire value or percentage of the rent.
This'll be supply.
So, availability, leasing velocity, and then, again, your exposure, lease, and occupancy.
And, again, you can export all these out.
So that's gonna be one functionality, just for the, kind of, operations.
Second one, you'll pop into Research.
This will just show you, like, how fast you can create these, market surveys for acquisition purposes or new development.
You just type in the property name or address, click on it here, and then, boom, right there.
You get a real-time market survey for any property in the country.
We will pull in what we believe to be the best 9 comps, but you and your teams, again, are in complete control of it, so you can edit those on the fly.
Last thing here is gonna be market sub-market level information.
I'm based out of Scottsdale, so I'll just pull up Phoenix.
Dude.
Some good golf over there.
Huh?
Oh, good old… yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.
A lot of golfers here.
I'm very much into, … I'm very much into, like, trail running and hiking, so that's kind of, like.
Nice.
And what my friends… yeah.
Yeah, I love it here, though, it's nice.
And we're maybe, like, a month out from actually having nice California weather for….
out.
So, we're looking forward.
Almost there.
Nice, dude.
So this will be market… sub-market level information.

So right now in Phoenix, we're tracking 2,000 properties, quarter of a million units, and we'll show you rent performance, year-over-year rent growth, and then what I like is we'll give you a 30-day snapshot.
We'll break everything down by the bed type.
And then if you'll notice on the map, it's interactive, so if you hover over any of the pins, we can give you more information on the stabilized asset or the construction project.
But there's 4 different colored pins.
Black is existing, yellow is under construction, purple is scheduled, turquoise is in planning, and that's gonna kind of be where the new supply data comes in.
So if we click into this, we could break this out by stage, so we'll show you each stage that they're in, the deliveries quarter over quarter.
And then, last thing here is gonna be demographics.
So, this is gonna be your population, education, income, and employment.
And, you can take this all the way down to the neighborhood.
Cool.

And, some nice filtering options here is, depending on what properties you're trying to take a look at, you could do presets for your built or renovated, minimum or maximum of units, you could search by management company, even ownership, property class, construction status, BTR, etc.
A lot of different ways to customize, kind of, what properties you want to show up in this report.
I know that was fast.
Any, any… I'm sure you got some questions, so fire them off.

No, no, I'm pretty familiar with, so, like, now, like, now I'm seeing it, I'm pretty familiar with, like, systems that kind of do stuff like this, … I think I want to say Appfolio just rolled something out that has something similar to something like this.

Not as, I would say, as detailed as you guys, but it's something, like, it's something that they're basically… they rolled out that does have, like, where you can do marketables and… rent trends, and all those kind of things, but I… I don't think it goes… it doesn't dive as deep as, you know, construct… new constructions, plannings, and all those kind of things, too, as well, or things that… or even properties where you're going to be taking over, so I think that's pretty cool, actually, on your guys' end.
But it looks good.
I like the way it looks, and I can… I can honestly understand why Camille and Amy probably got to the point of almost signing with you guys, like, that's… looks really good.

It looks like it's valuable information for any property management company, to be honest, so… so I know that's one thing that we definitely take pride in, and we assure that our teams are doing is market surveys, because gathering that data live is or, like, in person, and then transferring to something like this is… I can see the valuable… the value in it being insane, so….
Yeah.
I always like to say, the data's only as good as you go out and collect it.
Yeah.
A lot of calls, when they're doing it manually, they're like, oh, we do it once a month, and it's like, well, are you making pricing decisions?
Because on the units report, we'll track every time an individual unit makes pricing changes.
They're all on revenue management tools, they're changing prices every… Absolutely.
So….
Of course, yeah, yeah.
should be outdated, but … yeah, I like the… I appreciate the feedback.
Curious, … you think it would make sense for me to try to just shoot Camille a quick email, let her know that you and I.
Yeah.

Yeah, yeah, no, I would just probably, if anything, I would… you don't even have to even tell her that we connected, I would just probably just let her know, like, hey, Camille, just wanted to see, like, you know.
if you are still interested in possibly, like, you know, in our product, or if you wanted to kind of remap, because I'm not sure what she's doing when it comes to stuff like this.

Like I said, I'm not sure if we're… if they're gonna be fully adopting that folio thing that they rolling out, like, I'm really not 100% sure, just because when it comes to, like, market survey and analytics of that nature, it's more of, like, marketing, and she… and that she works directly with our president and, Amy when it comes to that kind of stuff, so… But, I mean, touching base with her, like, I think that would… that would be helpful.
Like, again, she may… she may be looking for something.
I'm not 100% sure.
Okay, cool.
Any, anything that you want me to kind of send you after the call?
No!
I think everything's good.
If you wanted to follow up with me, you're more than welcome to.

You know, I'm always available and all that kind of stuff for the majority of the time, so… but, I mean, the product looks good, and I think you did a great job presenting it, so, I mean, thanks, man.
I really appreciate the time that you took for me today.
Yeah, for sure.
Thanks, Matt.
Alright, honey.
Well, you take care, brother.
You as well.
Hopefully that weather gets better soon for your hiking.
Alright, take care, friend.
